description:
`fold_prim` at src/reducer.rs:188 explicitly skips `Prim::MakeList`:

> List structural folding requires IR-walking (RED.3+); the Descr lattice's `list_of(elem)` loses length info.

This is a real structural problem, not an oversight. The Descr lattice can't carry "list of length N with these specific elements" — `list_of(int)` is the most precise it gets. So unlike tuples/closures, list literal folding can't ride on top of fold_prim → materializer; it needs a different mechanism.

## Possible shapes

- A side-channel "literal list shape" carried alongside Descr in the env, populated by the reducer when it sees `Prim::MakeList(literal_vars, None)`.
- A richer Descr variant `list_lit(elems)` extending the lattice (heavyweight; affects subtyping).
- IR-walking inside the reducer: when folding a Term::Call, materialize literal list args by walking back to the MakeList stmt.

## Out of scope until designed

Sister/follow-up to the materializer epic (fz-f88) but not part of it — needs its own design pass.

## Surfaces

Any fn that returns a list with literal elements, called with literal args. E.g., `fn pair_list(a, b), do: [a, b]` — currently never folds.
title: fz: enable list literal folding (IR-walking per RED.3+ deferred comment)
